Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 [Read Email]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "NorthWind", "Pam Kowalski", or "PathFinder" (or similar) to find Pam's formal escalation and the NorthWind failure correspondence. Expected findings: Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning email to David Chen (email_email_7168baed8438, dated 2026-04-24) explicitly naming PathFinder Logistics as a competitor they are in preliminary conversations with, demanding a retention proposal by Friday, and citing the chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server move, the piano damage on Emilia Cruz's relocation, and Catalina's broken April 14 recovery plan promise.
OE 2 [Read Email]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Victor Huang", "chain-of-custody", "Raj Patel", or "security" (or similar) to find the chain-of-custody breach details. Expected findings: Victor Huang (NorthWind CISO) sent a furious email to Chloe Vance (email_email_f02adf9dc632) reporting that movers arrived 2 hours before the scheduled 9:00 AM IT escort, loaded without supervision, and servers were held overnight in a warehouse violating all chain-of-custody requirements. Victor's original security requirements referenced inline in his breach email ("my note below") and corroborated by the deal description (deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2) specified encryption verification, supervised loading with IT escort, direct transport with no warehouse stops, and a signed chain-of-custody log.
OE 3 [Read Email]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Steinway", "KeyMove", "Emilia Cruz", "piano", or "Craig Nguyen" (or similar) to find the piano damage claim details. Expected findings: Craig Nguyen at KeyMove Specialty Transport sent a damage report to Blessing Okafor documenting a 4.5–5 inch scratch on the bass side rim of Emilia Cruz's Steinway Model B grand piano during stairwell extraction at the second-floor landing (with six photos). KeyMove asks whether to open a formal insurance claim or hold pending client review. Additionally, a CRM engagement (crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c) logged an April 12 inbound call from Emilia Cruz about the piano scratch.
OE 4 [Read Email]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Catalina" and "NorthWind", or "recovery plan" (or similar) to find Catalina's April 14 promise and any follow-through. Expected findings: Catalina Dubois sent Pam Kowalski an email (email_email_ab22f67eeeb0, dated 2026-04-14) promising a "comprehensive service recovery plan within the week" covering the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown, the Emilia Cruz damage claim, and concrete service changes. No subsequent email from Catalina delivering this recovery plan is found confirming Pam's claim of non-delivery.
OE 5 [Read Airtable]: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records with queries related to "Raj Patel" and "Emilia Cruz" (or "NorthWind" or "Denver") to find the current relocation status. Expected findings: Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) Status: "In Progress", Chicago → Denver, Move dates 2026-04-21 to 2026-04-23, Assigned Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Special Requirements: server lab with chain-of-custody and hazmat UPS batteries. Emilia Cruz (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver) Status: "In Progress", Chicago → Denver, Move dates 2026-04-14 to 2026-04-18, Assigned Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Special Requirements: Steinway piano needing specialty subcontractor (piano subcontractor TBD), 27-day double-rent overlap.
OE 6 [Read Email/Slack]: Search emails and Slack using search_emails and conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Blessing" and "NorthWind" or "Denver" or "vendor" (or similar) to determine whether vendors are locked in for both Raj and Emilia. Expected findings: Raj Patel is assigned to Swift Relocations for the server move. Emilia Cruz is assigned to Heartland Movers for household goods and a piano specialty subcontractor (initially KeyMove or Gentle Giant) but the Airtable record shows "piano subcontractor TBD" and the CRM engagement note flags "no existing vendor agreement" for the piano mover. Vendors are not fully "locked in" piano vendor onboarding is an open risk.
OE 7 [Read Email]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Victor Huang" and "custody" or "documentation" (or similar) to determine whether Victor received the chain-of-custody documentation he required. Expected findings: Victor's security breach email demanded a written timeline, names of handlers, warehouse details, and an explanation for the escort miss. No email from MoveOps to Victor providing the requested documentation is found confirming a documentation gap. The Linear ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) and comments also note the documentation trail "cannot fully prove compliant handling after the fact."
OE 8 [Read Email/QuickBooks]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "KeyMove" or "piano claim" or "insurance" (or similar), and search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills with queries related to "KeyMove" to determine whether the piano claim is resolved. Expected findings: A QuickBooks bill exists (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417, $1,200, "Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction"). KeyMove's email asks whether to open a formal claim or hold no resolution email is found. The claim is still open / unresolved.
OE 9 [Read Email/Slack/CRM/Linear]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Catalina" and "NorthWind" (or similar), search Slack using conversations_search_messages for Catalina's NorthWind activity, search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements for NorthWind, and search Linear using linear_list_issues for Catalina's assigned tickets to check what Catalina has done on NorthWind since the escalation. Expected findings: Catalina sent the initial April 14 recovery plan promise but produced no actual recovery plan. No follow-up email from Catalina to Pam or Victor is found. No CRM engagement notes from Catalina after April 14. Catalina admitted capacity overload in #operations on April 14 ("I am drowning in logistics and can't keep up") but produced no follow-up emails, Slack messages, or CRM notes on NorthWind between April 14 and Pam's April 24 final warning. Pam ultimately bypassed Catalina entirely with her April 24 final warning sent directly to David.
OE 10 [Read Email]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Ridgeline", "Kenji Watanabe", or "Anya Petrova" (or similar) to find the referral chain and check whether Kenji replied. Expected findings: (a) Anya Petrova's warm intro email (email_email_722eaf59ac11, 2026-04-18) introducing Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin relocation batch of five employees, sent to David and CC'ing Mina. (b) Kenji's follow-up email (email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1, 2026-04-21) asking about a call. (c) David's belated reply (email_email_57faf42664a0, 2026-04-24) apologizing and proposing call times. (d) Mina's follow-up reply (email_email_a0cae8e27470, 2026-04-24) also reaching out to Kenji. (e) No reply from Kenji after David's or Mina's emails confirming the lead may be going cold.
OE 11 [Read CRM]: Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies with query "Ridgeline" (or similar), and search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with query "Ridgeline" (or similar), and search CRM leads using crm_search_leads for "Kenji Watanabe" (or similar) to check whether CRM records are properly filled out or placeholders. Expected findings: (a) Company record (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2): Ridgeline Robotics, Austin, TX, 180 employees, created 2026-04-24
has basic info but was created same day as David's rushed reply. (b) Lead record (crm_lead_de04045463c3): Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, referral from Anya Petrova, notes mention David replied 2026-04-24. (c) Deal record (crm_deal_a95b4721982a): "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)", $8,750, stage "Qualified To Buy", created 2026-04-24. Records exist but may need enrichment with current findings (no response from Kenji, referral still unanswered).
OE 12 [Read Linear]: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with queries related to "Ridgeline" (or similar) to check whether a dedicated tracking ticket exists. Expected findings: No standalone Ridgeline-specific ticket exists. Ridgeline is mentioned inside the StormCloud reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, #284: "StormCloud account incident reconstruction Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop"), with David's comment (linear_comment_e3a74d2d5108) confirming he created CRM objects and replied to Kenji. However, there is no dedicated ticket for tracking the Ridgeline lead separately from the StormCloud incident.
OE 13 [Read Slack]: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Ridgeline" or "Kenji" (or similar) to find David's Slack celebration and team context. Expected findings: David Chen's message in #customer-engagement (C002, 2026-04-19) celebrating the StormCloud momentum and Anya's warm intro to Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin batch of 5, confirming he made a big deal about it. Also find any additional team context about the delayed response.
OE 14 [Read CRM]: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with queries related to "NorthWind" (or similar) and check engagements using crm_list_engagements for NorthWind company to find current deal status and engagement history. Expected findings: NorthWind company record (company_northwind), Denver expansion deal(s) with details about Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz relocations, and engagement notes including the recovery context note (eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context) documenting Raj assigned to Swift, Emilia to Heartland + piano vendor with onboarding risk.
OE 15 [Read Linear]: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with queries related to "NorthWind" or "retention" (or similar) to find any existing retention tracking ticket. Expected findings: Issue #482 (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1): "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority 1, assigned to David Chen, due 2026-04-24, with multiple comments from Chloe Vance (ops timeline reconstruction), Alejandro Fuentes (draft retention pricing model), David Chen (cross-system analysis connecting Raj control failure + Emilia care failure + Catalina's broken promise + PathFinder threat).
OE 16 [Read Contacts]: Look up contact details for Pam Kowalski using contacts_search_contacts or search_emails to find her email address. Expected finding: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com.
OE 17 [Read Contacts]: Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts. Expected finding: elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 18 [Read Contacts]: Look up Kenji Watanabe's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query "Kenji Watanabe" (or similar). Expected finding: kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com. Note: A different Kenji Watanabe exists at Driftwood Analytics (k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com) agent must resolve to the correct one.
OE 19 [Write CRM]: Update CRM notes on the NorthWind deal using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or similar, linked to NorthWind company_id) with findings from the investigation: chain-of-custody breach confirmed (Victor Huang's security report, movers arrived 2hrs early, overnight warehouse storage), Steinway piano damage unresolved (KeyMove $1,200 claim open), Catalina's April 14 recovery plan never delivered, Pam's formal April 24 final warning naming PathFinder as competitor, current Denver relocation status for both Raj and Emilia, and the documentation gap for Victor's chain-of-custody requirements.
OE 20 [Write CRM]: Flesh out Ridgeline Robotics CRM records using crm_create_engagement (linked to Ridgeline company) with updated findings: Kenji Watanabe has not replied to either David's (April 24) or Mina's (April 24) emails, lead is at risk of going cold, referral source is Anya Petrova at StormCloud (existing client), 5-employee Austin relocation batch, follow-up email being sent.
OE 21 [Write Linear]: Update the existing NorthWind retention response ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, #482) using linear_create_comment with current information: Pam's April 24 final warning received naming PathFinder competitor, email sent to Pam acknowledging failures and proposing call, Elena CC'd, team sync scheduled for Thursday to walk through retention plan, outstanding items include Victor's unanswered chain-of-custody documentation request and unresolved KeyMove piano claim.
OE 22 [Write Linear]: Create a dedicated Ridgeline Robotics tracking ticket using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or similar, title related to "Ridgeline Robotics referral follow-up" or similar, description covering: warm referral from Anya Petrova at StormCloud on April 18, Kenji Watanabe HR Director for 5-employee Austin batch, initial response delayed until April 24, Kenji has not replied to David's or Mina's outreach, follow-up email sent, CRM records created and updated, priority follow-up needed due to referral sensitivity and StormCloud account risk context).
OE 23 [Write Email]: Send email to Pam Kowalski using send_email from david.chen@moveops.com to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, CC elena.rostova@moveops.com. The email should: (a) acknowledge specifically what went wrong the chain-of-custody protocol breach on Raj Patel's server move (movers loaded without IT escort, overnight warehouse storage), the Steinway piano damage on Emilia Cruz's relocation, and the broken recovery plan commitment from April 14; (b) lay out corrective actions for the Denver moves current vendor status, steps being taken to resolve the chain-of-custody documentation gap, and KeyMove claim resolution path; (c) ask Pam if she'll take a call before making a final vendor decision. The tone should not make excuses.
OE 24 [Write Email]: Conditionally send a follow-up email to Kenji Watanabe only if investigation confirms Kenji has not replied to David's or Mina's email (which the data confirms). Send using send_email from david.chen@moveops.com to kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com. The email should reinforce that MoveOps is serious about Anya Petrova's referral from StormCloud and ask whether Ridgeline is still evaluating relocation partners for the Austin batch.
OE 25 [Write Slack]: Post a status update in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message covering both accounts so Mina and Emeka have context. The message should include: (a) NorthWind status Pam's final warning received, PathFinder competitor threat, chain-of-custody breach and piano damage unresolved, email sent to Pam with Elena CC'd, retention plan in progress; (b) Ridgeline status referral from Anya Petrova at StormCloud, Kenji has not replied after David's and Mina's outreach, follow-up sent, CRM updated, tracking ticket created; (c) team sync scheduled for Thursday.
OE 26 [Write Calendar]: Schedule a team sync meeting on Thursday (April 30, 2026) using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "NorthWind retention plan sync" or "Team sync NorthWind / Ridgeline" (or similar), with attendees including relevant team members (david.chen@moveops.com, and potentially mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com, or others), to walk through the retention plan together.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1 [Read Email]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "NorthWind", "Pam Kowalski", or "PathFinder" (or similar) to find Pam's formal escalation and the NorthWind failure correspondence. Expected findings: Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning email to David Chen (email_email_7168baed8438, dated 2026-04-24) explicitly naming PathFinder Logistics as a competitor they are in preliminary conversations with, demanding a retention proposal by Friday, and citing the chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server move, the piano damage on Emilia Cruz's relocation, and Catalina's broken April 14 recovery plan promise.
OE 2 [Read Email]: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Victor Huang", "chain-of-custody", "Raj Patel", or "security" (or similar) to find the chain-of-custody breach details. Expected findings: Victor Huang (NorthWind CISO) sent a furious email to Chloe Vance (email_email_f02adf9dc632) reporting that movers arrived 2 hours before the scheduled 9:00 AM IT escort, loaded without supervision, and servers were held overnight in a warehouse violating all chain-of-custody requirements. Victor's original security requirements referenced inline in his breach email ("my note below") and corroborated by the deal description (deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2) specified encryption verification, supervised loading with IT escort, direct transport with no warehouse stops, and a signed chain-of-custody log.

KeyMove asks whether to open a formal insurance claim or hold pending client review. (b) Kenji's follow-up email (email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1, 2026-04-21) asking about a call. (c) David's belated reply (email_email_57faf42664a0, 2026-04-24) apologizing and proposing call times. (d) Mina's follow-up reply (email_email_a0cae8e27470, 2026-04-24) also reaching out to Kenji. (e) No reply from Kenji after David's or Mina's emails confirming the lead may be going cold.
OE 11 [Read CRM]: Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies with query "Ridgeline" (or similar), and search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with query "Ridgeline" (or similar), and search CRM leads using crm_search_leads for "Kenji Watanabe" (or similar) to check whether CRM records are properly filled out or placeholders. Expected findings: (a) Company record (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2): Ridgeline Robotics, Austin, TX, 180 employees, created 2026-04-24
has basic info but was created same day as David's rushed reply. (b) Lead record (crm_lead_de04045463c3): Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, referral from Anya Petrova, notes mention David replied 2026-04-24. (c) Deal record (crm_deal_a95b4721982a): "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)", $8,750, stage "Qualified To Buy", created 2026-04-24. Records exist but may need enrichment with current findings (no response from Kenji, referral still unanswered).
OE 12 [Read Linear]: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with queries related to "Ridgeline" (or similar) to check whether a dedicated tracking ticket exists. Expected findings: No standalone Ridgeline-specific ticket exists. Ridgeline is mentioned inside the StormCloud reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, #284: "StormCloud account incident reconstruction Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop"), with David's comment (linear_comment_e3a74d2d5108) confirming he created CRM objects and replied to Kenji. However, there is no dedicated ticket for tracking the Ridgeline lead separately from the StormCloud incident.
OE 13 [Read Slack]: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Ridgeline" or "Kenji" (or similar) to find David's Slack celebration and team context. Expected findings: David Chen's message in #customer-engagement (C002, 2026-04-19) celebrating the StormCloud momentum and Anya's warm intro to Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin batch of 5, confirming he made a big deal about it. Also find any additional team context about the delayed response.
OE 14 [Read CRM]: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with queries related to "NorthWind" (or similar) and check engagements using crm_list_engagements for NorthWind company to find current deal status and engagement history. Expected findings: NorthWind company record (company_northwind), Denver expansion deal(s) with details about Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz relocations, and engagement notes including the recovery context note (eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context) documenting Raj assigned to Swift, Emilia to Heartland + piano vendor with onboarding risk.
OE 15 [Read Linear]: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with queries related to "NorthWind" or "retention" (or similar) to find any existing retention tracking ticket. Expected findings: Issue #482 (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1): "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority 1, assigned to David Chen, due 2026-04-24, with multiple comments from Chloe Vance (ops timeline reconstruction), Alejandro Fuentes (draft retention pricing model), David Chen (cross-system analysis connecting Raj control failure + Emilia care failure + Catalina's broken promise + PathFinder threat).
OE 16 [Read Contacts]: Look up contact details for Pam Kowalski using contacts_search_contacts or search_emails to find her email address. Expected finding: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com.
OE 17 [Read Contacts]: Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts. Expected finding: elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 18 [Read Contacts]: Look up Kenji Watanabe's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query "Kenji Watanabe" (or similar). Expected finding: kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com. Note: A different Kenji Watanabe exists at Driftwood Analytics (k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com) agent must resolve to the correct one.
OE 19 [Write CRM]: Update CRM notes on the NorthWind deal using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or similar, linked to NorthWind company_id) with findings from the investigation: chain-of-custody breach confirmed (Victor Huang's security report, movers arrived 2hrs early, overnight warehouse storage), Steinway piano damage unresolved (KeyMove $1,200 claim open), Catalina's April 14 recovery plan never delivered, Pam's formal April 24 final warning naming PathFinder as competitor, current Denver relocation status for both Raj and Emilia, and the documentation gap for Victor's chain-of-custody requirements.
OE 20 [Write CRM]: Flesh out Ridgeline Robotics CRM records using crm_create_engagement (linked to Ridgeline company) with updated findings: Kenji Watanabe has not replied to either David's (April 24) or Mina's (April 24) emails, lead is at risk of going cold, referral source is Anya Petrova at StormCloud (existing client), 5-employee Austin relocation batch, follow-up email being sent.
OE 21 [Write Linear]: Update the existing NorthWind retention response ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, #482) using linear_create_comment with current information: Pam's April 24 final warning received naming PathFinder competitor, email sent to Pam acknowledging failures and proposing call, Elena CC'd, team sync scheduled for Thursday to walk through retention plan, outstanding items include Victor's unanswered chain-of-custody documentation request and unresolved KeyMove piano claim.
OE 22 [Write Linear]: Create a dedicated Ridgeline Robotics tracking ticket using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or similar, title related to "Ridgeline Robotics referral follow-up" or similar, description covering: warm referral from Anya Petrova at StormCloud on April 18, Kenji Watanabe HR Director for 5-employee Austin batch, initial response delayed until April 24, Kenji has not replied to David's or Mina's outreach, follow-up email sent, CRM records created and updated, priority follow-up needed due to referral sensitivity and StormCloud account risk context).
OE 23 [Write Email]: Send email to Pam Kowalski using send_email from david.chen@moveops.com to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, CC elena.rostova@moveops.com. The email should: (a) acknowledge specifically what went wrong the chain-of-custody protocol breach on Raj Patel's server move (movers loaded without IT escort, overnight warehouse storage), the Steinway piano damage on Emilia Cruz's relocation, and the broken recovery plan commitment from April 14; (b) lay out corrective actions for the Denver moves current vendor status, steps being taken to resolve the chain-of-custody documentation gap, and KeyMove claim resolution path; (c) ask Pam if she'll take a call before making a final vendor decision. The tone should not make excuses.
OE 24 [Write Email]: Conditionally send a follow-up email to Kenji Watanabe only if investigation confirms Kenji has not replied to David's or Mina's email (which the data confirms). Send using send_email from david.chen@moveops.com to kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com. The email should reinforce that MoveOps is serious about Anya Petrova's referral from StormCloud and ask whether Ridgeline is still evaluating relocation partners for the Austin batch.
OE 25 [Write Slack]: Post a status update in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message covering both accounts so Mina and Emeka have context. The message should include: (a) NorthWind status Pam's final warning received, PathFinder competitor threat, chain-of-custody breach and piano damage unresolved, email sent to Pam with Elena CC'd, retention plan in progress; (b) Ridgeline status referral from Anya Petrova at StormCloud, Kenji has not replied after David's and Mina's outreach, follow-up sent, CRM updated, tracking ticket created; (c) team sync scheduled for Thursday.
OE 26 [Write Calendar]: Schedule a team sync meeting on Thursday (April 30, 2026) using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "NorthWind retention plan sync" or "Team sync NorthWind / Ridgeline" (or similar), with attendees including relevant team members (david.chen@moveops.com, and potentially mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com, or others), to walk through the retention plan together.

✗Run #6The Airtable record for Emilia Cruz clearly shows 'piano subcontractor TBD' and 'NO existing vendor agreement' for the piano mover. However, the agent's final response states 'Emilia's piano vendor was KeyMove' — treating KeyMove as the confirmed vendor based on Slack damage reports. The response never mentions that the piano specialty subcontractor was TBD or that vendor onboarding was incomplete, missing this critical open risk.

tool selection
